title: hieracium racemosum subsp. neocroceum
intro: Hieracium racemosum subsp. neocroceum, also known as Hawkweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Hieracium racemosum subsp. neocroceum are often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
